title: Annual Report 2022
summary: This is the Bank of Canada's Annual Report 2022 landing page, containing only a table of contents and minimal descriptive text. The extracted content confirms that Canadian inflation rose from just under 5% to 8% in June 2022 before falling to just over 6% by year-end, and that the Bank undertook its most rapid tightening cycle in Canada's history. The substantive content of the annual report is not present in this extraction.
key_points: Source is a landing page/table of contents for the full Annual Report 2022, not the actual report content
Canadian inflation rose from just under 5% at the start of 2022 to 8% in June 2022, then fell to just over 6% by year-end
The Bank of Canada undertook the most rapid tightening cycle in Canada's history during 2022

Report sections listed include: mandate and planning framework, monetary policy, financial system, retail payments supervision, funds management, currency, communications, global engagement, governance, risk management, and financial statements
Actual substantive analysis and data from the report are not included in this extraction
why_it_matters: This source is too thin to provide actionable research value. It is a navigation page for the Bank of Canada Annual Report 2022, not the report itself. The only substantive information is high-level inflation trajectory and the characterization of the 2022 tightening cycle as the most rapid in Canadian history.
macro_context: Historical record of Canadian inflation trajectory in 2022 and Bank of Canada monetary policy response. The data confirms inflation peaked at 8% in June 2022 and retreated to just over 6% by year-end, with the Bank responding through aggressive rate hikes.
